rulings.Case Title: 1068 FLORIDA LLC VS. SEOSAMH O'BRIAIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 23, 2020 line 1. DEFENDANTs RODRIGO SANTOS, SANTOS & URRUTIA ASSOCIATES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you personally appear for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing all parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. Defendant's Demurer to First Amended Complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the first cause of action for fraud is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of each element of this cause of action including representation with intent to defraud resulting in damage and Plaintiff's reliance. Demurrer is sustained to the allegations re: conspiracy for plaintiff to allege facts supporting conspiracy. Demurrer is overruled as to the cause of action for conversion. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN F. LEE VS. DANIEL EVERETT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 23, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT DANIEL EVERETT's Motion To Vacate Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you personally appear for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing all parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. Defendant's Motion to Vacate. is GRANTED, the operative notice has not been properly served. See Proof of Service attached to the Complaint dated April 3, 2019, Declaration of Garry Preneta dated May 14, 2019, CCP 1162 and Liebovich v. Shahrokhkhany (1997) 56 Cal.App.4th 511. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ANTON HALTEH VS. DAVID MORGANSTERN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 23, 2020 line 3. DEFENDANT DAVID MORGANSTERN's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you personally appear for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing all parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. Defendant's Demurrer to Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint does not constitute a sham pleading, and thus, Exhibit 2 to the Complaint (which includes the 3-Day Notice and Form 1007) should not be disregarded. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: HAPPY 42ND AVE PARTNERS LLC VS. JODY TELLIER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 23, 2020 line 4. PLAINTIFF HAPPY 42ND AVE PARTNERS LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you personally appear for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing all parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden. No competent admissible evidence provided to show that Defendant remains in possession of the premises. Declaration of Zahra Rezain is insufficient. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JASPER V. LUPPINO VS. CHAU TRAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash And Motion To Quash
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 23, 2020 line 6. DEFENDANT PHAT TRAN's Motion To Quash Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you personally appear for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing all parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. Defendant's Motion to Quash is GRANTED. Emergency rule was adopted "effective April 6, 2020," without any indication in the rule that it was to be effective only for the part of the day, i.e. does not cover the whole day on April 6, 2020. While it is possible that the Summons issued prior to the Rule being adopted on April 6, 2020, this technical reading of the rules is not supported by any authority in opposition. Plaintiff may apply for issuance of summons under Emergency Rule 1. = (501/CFH)
